name: Customer Support Triage
trigger: New support email or manually assigned request
steps: Classify request
Search approved knowledge
Draft response
QA review
Human approval
Create or send response

name: Feature Intake to Engineering
trigger: New customer feedback or founder request
steps: Summarise request
Find related evidence
Draft requirements
Architecture review
Draft implementation plan and technical tasks
QA acceptance criteria
Founder approval
Create GitHub issue

name: Daily Command Brief
steps: Collect overdue work
Review risks and approvals
Summarise customer signals
Prepare decision list
Publish internal brief

name: Release Readiness Gate
trigger: Pull request marked ready for release
steps: Check approved scope and acceptance criteria
Review pull request and implementation evidence
Run test, lint, build, and security checks
Review migration and rollback impact
Collect required approvals
Record release decision

name: Growth Campaign Lifecycle
trigger: Approved growth objective, product launch, or campaign request
steps: Define audience and measurable objective
Research search demand, market, and competitors
Draft positioning, content, email, and sales enablement assets
Validate claims, consent, data use, and brand requirements
Review budget and channel plan
Human approval
Launch through approved integrations
Measure attribution, conversion, and pipeline impact
approval: Required before external launch, spend, bulk send, or commercial commitment
sla: Configured by campaign priority and launch date

name: Compliance Assurance Lifecycle
trigger: New product, process, vendor, policy, incident, audit request, or material operational change
steps: Identify applicable obligations and control owners
Classify data, regulated activity, risk, and evidence requirements
Assess current controls and document gaps
Draft DPIA, policy, audit evidence, or remediation actions
Validate with product, security, operations, and accountable human owners
Obtain approval for protected decisions and regulatory commitments
Record evidence, owners, due dates, residual risk, and review schedule
Monitor remediation and re-assess after material change
approval: Required for formal sign-off, policy publication, risk acceptance, regulatory communication, or sensitive-data action
sla: Configured by regulatory deadline, risk severity, and audit commitment

name: Development Delivery Lifecycle
trigger: Approved GitHub issue, technical task, defect, or remediation request
steps: Confirm approved scope, acceptance criteria, architecture, and risk controls
Inspect existing code, dependencies, schema, and related tests
Create isolated branch and implementation plan
Implement the smallest complete vertical increment
Run unit, integration, lint, type, build, accessibility, and security checks
Prepare pull request with evidence, migration notes, and rollback plan
Obtain QA, security, architecture, compliance, and human approvals as required
Merge and deploy only through approved automation
Verify production health and record audit evidence
approval: Required for production, destructive, security-sensitive, paid-service, or externally committed changes
sla: Configured by priority, release class, and incident severity
